title: Backslash escapes in keyId quoted string not handled: parser bypass
description:
server.rs:769-798 (extract_key_id_from_sig) and signatures.rs:128-149 (split_signature_params): both parsers toggle in_quotes on every '"' character but do not handle backslash escapes.

RFC 7230 quoted-string allows `\" ` as an escaped double-quote inside a quoted value. The parser would see `\"` and toggle out of quote mode, truncating the keyId at the backslash-escaped quote.

Example attack header:
`Signature: keyId="https://attacker.example/\",signature="AABB"`

Parser behavior:
- Sees opening quote after keyId=
- Sees \", toggles out of in_quotes (now thinks it's outside quotes)
- The comma after / is treated as a separator
- keyId extracted: `https://attacker.example/\`
- Then `signature=` in the second part

This could be used to craft a keyId that the parser misinterprets, potentially pointing to a different host than what a compliant parser would derive. In practice the exploitability depends on whether the mangled keyId is still a reachable URL, but the parser non-compliance is a correctness issue and a potential source of parser-differential bugs.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
